records.ety: [Prov. It. macaroni, It. maccheroni, fr. Gr. <?/ happiness, later, a funeral feast, fr. <?/ blessed, happy. Prob. so called because eaten at such feasts in honor of the dead; cf. Gr. <?/ blessed, i. e., dead. Cf. Macaroon.]

defs: Long slender tubes made of a paste chiefly of a wheat flour such as semolina, and used as an article of food; a form of Italian pasta.
notes: ☞ A paste similarly prepared is largely used as food in Persia, India, and China, but is not commonly made tubular like the Italian macaroni.

defs: A medley; something droll or extravagant.
A sort of droll or fool.
A finical person; a fop; -- applied especially to English fops of about 1775, who affected the mannerisms and clothing of continental Europe.
The designation of a body of Maryland soldiers in the Revolutionary War, distinguished by a rich uniform.
